title: What to know about Paeonia lactiflora before using it medicinally
intro_desc: Paeonia lactiflora, commonly known as peony, is a flowering plant with a long history of medicinal use. The herb is traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and menstrual disorders. Its main health benefits include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. Paeonia lactiflora contains therapeutic properties such as antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and sedative effects. The herb is rich in bioactive compounds like paeoniflorin, paeonol, and benzaldehyde, which are often used to make herbal preparations like tea and tinctures.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Journal of ethnopharmacology, the health benefits of Zingiber officinale include its ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate fever, as evidenced by its reduction of carrageenan-induced paw swelling and yeast-induced fever in rats. Additionally, the extract showed antimicrobial properties by inhibiting the growth of both Gram-positive and Gram-negative bacteria. Furthermore, Zingiber officinale has been found to have hypoglycaemic effects, lowering blood glucose levels in rabbits.
uses_description: The main medicinal uses of Paeonia lactiflora are fever reduction, due to the presence of isoquinoline alkaloids, which have antipyretic properties; menstrual cramps relief, attributed to the flavonoids and phenolic acids that exhibit anti-inflammatory and spasmolytic effects; and rheumatism treatment, facilitated by the glycosides that possess anti-inflammatory and analgesic activities. Paeonia lactiflora is also employed to alleviate anxiety, as the flavonoids and phenolic acids in the plant have anxiolytic properties. Additionally, this plant is used to treat menstrual disorders and hysteria, the latter likely due to the sedative effects of the isoquinoline alkaloids and flavonoids. Furthermore, Paeonia lactiflora has been used to treat epilepsy, depression, dysmenorrhea, and migraine, the latter two conditions possibly alleviated by the flavonoids and phenolic acids that exhibit anti-inflammatory and spasmolytic effects. The plant's various constituents, including glycosides, isoquinoline alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, work synergistically to provide relief from these conditions.

uses_list.description: This plant is used to treat fever due to its anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with fever.
The root of this plant has been traditionally used to treat anxiety and nervousness due to its sedative properties, which help to calm the nervous system and promote relaxation.

This plant is used to treat menstrual cramps and other symptoms associated with PMS, such as bloating and mood swings, due to its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which help to relax the uterine muscles and reduce pain.

The roots and rhizomes of this plant have been traditionally used to treat rheumatism and other musculoskeletal disorders due to their anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help to reduce pain and inflammation in the joints and muscles.
This plant has been traditionally used to treat depression due to its sedative and antidepressant properties, which help to calm the nervous system and promote a sense of well-being.

The root of this plant has been traditionally used to treat menstrual disorders, such as irregular menstrual cycles and heavy bleeding, due to its estrogenic and antispasmodic properties, which help to regulate the menstrual cycle and reduce uterine cramping.

This plant has been traditionally used to treat hysteria and other nervous disorders due to its sedative and antispasmodic properties, which help to calm the nervous system and reduce anxiety and restlessness.
The root of this plant has been traditionally used to treat epilepsy due to its anticonvulsant properties, which help to prevent seizures and reduce the frequency and severity of epileptic attacks.

This plant is used to treat dysmenorrhea and other symptoms associated with menstrual cramps, due to its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which help to relax the uterine muscles and reduce pain.

The root of this plant has been traditionally used to treat migraine and other types of headaches due to its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help to reduce pain and inflammation in the head and neck.

uses_modern_western_medicine_description: In modern western medicine, Paeonia lactiflora is used to treat a variety of conditions, including anxiety, which has been shown to be effectively managed with the plant's ability to regulate the body's stress response. Additionally, Paeonia lactiflora has been found to be effective in managing depression, as it has been shown to have a positive effect on neurotransmitter levels in the brain. The plant has also been used for pain management, with studies indicating that it can reduce inflammation and alleviate pain in patients with various conditions. Furthermore, Paeonia lactiflora has been used to alleviate menopausal symptoms, including menopausal hot flashes, and menstrual cramps, which can be debilitating for some women. The plant has also been studied for its potential use in treating epilepsy, insomnia, and sleep disorders, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects on these conditions.
uses_traditional_chinese_medicine_descri: In traditional chinese medicine, Paeonia lactiflora is used to treat fever by inducing sweating and relieving heat, anxiety by calming the spirit and soothing the nervous system, menstrual disorders by regulating the menstrual cycle, and dysmenorrhea by relieving abdominal pain and promoting blood flow. It also treats menstrual pain by reducing spasms and inflammation, as well as rheumatism by reducing joint inflammation and swelling. Furthermore, Paeonia lactiflora is used to treat inflammation by reducing swelling and promoting healing, and cough by moistening the lungs and relieving bronchial spasms. Additionally, it treats pneumonia by promoting expectoration and relieving lung congestion, and hysteria by regulating the emotional balance and calming the nervous system. Its therapeutic properties make it a valuable component in traditional chinese medicine.
uses_ayurvedic_medicine_description: In ayurvedic medicine, Paeonia lactiflora is used to treat fever by reducing its severity and promoting a quick recovery. It is also utilized to alleviate anxiety through its adaptogenic properties that help balance the body's response to stress. Furthermore, Paeonia lactiflora has been found to be effective in treating menstrual disorders, including conditions such as dysmenorrhea and irregular menstrual cycles. The plant's anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties make it a valuable treatment for rheumatism, menstrual cramps, and inflammation. Additionally, Paeonia lactiflora has been used to treat digestive issues, rheumatic arthritis, hysteria, and even epilepsy, showcasing its broad range of therapeutic applications.
uses_unani_medicine_description: In unani medicine, Paeonia lactiflora is used to treat fever by inducing sweating and reducing body temperature, it is also used to treat hysteria by calming the nervous system, as well as rheumatism due to its anti-inflammatory properties. Additionally, it is used to treat anxiety by reducing stress and promoting relaxation. Paeonia lactiflora is also effective in treating epilepsy, where it helps to reduce the frequency and severity of seizures. It is also used to treat migraine by reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation. Furthermore, it is used to treat menorrhagia, dysmenorrhea and menstrual disorders by regulating menstrual cycles and reducing symptoms associated with menstruation.
uses_homeopathic_medicine_description: In homeopathic medicine, Paeonia lactiflora is used to treat menstrual cramps by reducing uterine contractions and easing pain. Its calming effects also help alleviate anxiety and promote relaxation. Additionally, it is used to alleviate symptoms associated with premenstrual syndrome, including mood swings and irritability. Furthermore, Paeonia lactiflora has anti-inflammatory properties that help in treating endometriosis by reducing inflammation and pain. This herb is also used to treat depression, menopause symptoms, migraines, epilepsy, hysteria, and insomnia by modulating neurotransmitters and hormones, thereby promoting emotional balance and relaxation.
benefits_description: Paeonia lactiflora inhibits inflammation by containing salicylic acid, which reduces the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes. Paeonia lactiflora reduces oxidative stress by scavenging free radicals and increasing the activity of antioxidant enzymes, protecting cells from damage. Paeonia lactiflora prevents cancer cell growth by inducing apoptosis and inhibiting cell proliferation, suppressing the expression of genes involved in tumor formation. Paeonia lactiflora improves cardiovascular health by reducing blood pressure and triglycerides, and preventing the formation of blood clots. Paeonia lactiflora reduces joint pain by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and reducing the activity of enzymes involved in joint destruction, while also supporting digestive health by regulating gut motility and reducing inflammation in the gastrointestinal tract, and reduces anxiety symptoms by modulating the activity of neurotransmitters, improving skin conditions by reducing the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and promoting wound healing, and prevents neurodegenerative diseases by reducing the formation of amyloid plaques and promoting the clearance of amyloid beta, and reduces menstrual disorders by regulating the menstrual cycle and reducing pain associated with dysmenorrhea.

benefits_list.description: This benefit is due to the presence of various bioactive compounds, including phenolic acids and polysaccharides, which have been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines, thereby reducing inflammation and alleviating associated symptoms.

The high content of antioxidants in this plant, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, helps to neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress, thereby protecting cells from damage and promoting overall health.

The bioactive compounds present in this plant, such as paeoniflorin and benzylisoquinoline alkaloids, have been shown to exhibit anti-cancer properties by inhibiting the growth and proliferation of cancer cells, inducing apoptosis, and suppressing angiogenesis.

The flavonoids and phenolic acids present in this plant have been shown to have vasodilatory, anti-thrombotic, and anti-hypertensive effects, thereby improving cardiovascular health and reducing the risk of cardiovascular diseases.

The analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties of the bioactive compounds present in this plant, including paeoniflorin and benzylisoquinoline alkaloids, help to reduce joint pain and inflammation, thereby alleviating symptoms associated with arthritis and other joint-related disorders.

The bioactive compounds present in this plant, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, thereby supporting digestive health and reducing the risk of gastrointestinal disorders.

The bioactive compounds present in this plant, including paeoniflorin and benzylisoquinoline alkaloids, have been shown to have anxiolytic and sedative effects, thereby reducing anxiety symptoms and promoting relaxation and calmness.

The antioxidants and bioactive compounds present in this plant, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and anti-aging effects, thereby improving skin conditions and reducing the risk of skin-related disorders.

The bioactive compounds present in this plant, including paeoniflorin and benzylisoquinoline alkaloids, have been shown to have neuroprotective effects, thereby preventing neurodegenerative diseases and reducing the risk of cognitive decline and dementia.

The bioactive compounds present in this plant, including paeoniflorin and benzylisoquinoline alkaloids, have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and anti-spasmodic effects, thereby reducing menstrual pain and cramping, and alleviating symptoms associated with menstrual disorders.

benefits_circulatory_system_description: Paeonia lactiflora improves blood flow by containing flavonoids that exhibit vasodilatory properties, allowing the plant to enhance vasodilation in blood vessels. Paeonia lactiflora lowers blood pressure through the presence of paeoniflorin, an anti-inflammatory compound that reduces inflammation in the cardiovascular system, thereby relaxing blood vessels. The antioxidant properties of Paeonia lactiflora increase oxygenation of the blood, while its anti-inflammatory properties reduce inflammation, allowing the plant to soothe nerves affected by cardiovascular issues. Paeonia lactiflora boosts circulation due to its ability to improve endothelial function, which is attributed to its content of saponins that stabilize heartbeat and improve blood vessel health. Additionally, Paeonia lactiflora's anti-inflammatory properties, such as those found in paeoniflorin, also contribute to its ability to lower blood pressure and enhance vasodilation in blood vessels.
benefits_digestive_system_description: Paeonia lactiflora reduces inflammation due to its anti-inflammatory properties, which help alleviate swelling and pain in the digestive system. This plant heals ulcers by exhibiting antimicrobial properties, inhibiting the growth of pathogenic bacteria that cause ulcers. Paeonia lactiflora soothes irritation with its antioxidant properties, protecting the digestive tract from oxidative stress caused by free radicals. The roots of Paeonia lactiflora relax muscles with its sedative properties, reducing spasms in the intestines and promoting a smooth digestive process. By exhibiting demulcent properties, Paeonia lactiflora improves digestion, tones intestines, promotes regularity, eases constipation, prevents diarrhea, and fights infections, making it a valuable herb for maintaining a healthy digestive system.
benefits_endocrine_system_description: Paeonia lactiflora regulates hormones through its estrogenic properties, which is responsible for its ability to balance the endocrine system. Paeonia lactiflora supports thyroid function due to its flavonoid content, which has been shown to enhance the conversion of T4 to T3. Paeonia lactiflora balances adrenal output through its adaptogenic properties, reducing the production of cortisol and promoting a state of homeostasis. Paeonia lactiflora stabilizes insulin levels by acting as an insulin sensitizer, reducing blood sugar levels and improving glucose metabolism. Paeonia lactiflora enhances ovarian function and promotes testicular health through its antioxidant properties, regenerates pancreatic tissue, improves menstrual cycles, boosts growth hormones, and reduces cortisol production.
benefits_integumentary_system_descriptio: Paeonia lactiflora heal wounds by exerting antimicrobial properties that inhibit the growth of pathogens, while its anti-inflammatory properties reduce inflammation and soothe irritations, promoting faster healing and preventing scarring of the integumentary system. The antioxidant properties of Paeonia lactiflora protect skin from oxidative stress and enhance elasticity, improving moisturizing and reducing redness caused by inflammation. Additionally, Paeonia lactiflora contains compounds that stabilize tissue, preventing further damage and promoting the healing process. Furthermore, Paeonia lactiflora's ability to reduce inflammation and soothe irritations also plays a crucial role in protecting skin from external factors and promoting faster healing. By combining these properties, Paeonia lactiflora provides comprehensive benefits for the integumentary system, making it an effective treatment for various skin-related issues.
benefits_lymphatic_system_description: Paeonia lactiflora stimulates lymph flow by activating lymphatic vessels due to its vasodilatory and anti-inflammatory properties. This action reduces swelling quickly, making it an effective remedy for edema and fluid retention. Paeonia lactiflora improves lymph function by enhancing the removal of waste products and toxins, which in turn promotes detoxification process. The antimicrobial and antioxidant properties of Paeonia lactiflora enhance lymph drainage, supporting lymph node health and fighting off infections. By accelerating wound healing, boosting immune system, increasing white blood cells, and supporting the body's natural defense mechanisms, Paeonia lactiflora plays a crucial role in maintaining a healthy lymphatic system.
benefits_musculoskeletal_system_descript: Paeonia lactiflora relieves joint pain due to its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which are primarily attributed to the presence of glycosides, alkaloids, and flavonoids. Paeonia lactiflora reduces inflammation through the inhibition of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines, thereby providing relief from joint pain and swelling. Paeonia lactiflora soothes muscle tension by exerting a sedative effect on the nervous system, which is a result of its flavonoids and alkaloids acting on the GABA receptors. Paeonia lactiflora eases arthritis symptoms by suppressing the production of inflammatory mediators and promoting the production of anti-inflammatory cytokines, thereby reducing joint damage and inflammation. Paeonia lactiflora prevents osteoporosis through the inhibition of osteoclast activity and the promotion of osteoblast activity, which is a result of its flavonoids and glycosides acting on the bone-forming and bone-resorbing cells.
benefits_nervous_system_description: Paeonia lactiflora reduces anxiety by containing flavonoids, which act as anxiolytics, and alkaloids, which possess a sedative effect. This plant relaxes nerves due to its neuroprotective properties, shielding neurons from damage and preserving their function. Paeonia lactiflora calms stress through its ability to modulate the hypothalamic-pituitary-adrenal axis, thereby regulating cortisol levels. Additionally, Paeonia lactiflora improves mood by exhibiting antidepressant properties, attributed to its phenolic acids and terpenoids. The plant also supports sleep by containing melatonin, which regulates the body's circadian rhythms, while reducing inflammation through its anti-inflammatory properties, and protecting neurons from oxidative damage, thus decreasing fatigue and enhancing focus.
benefits_reproductive_system_description: Paeonia lactiflora regulates menstruation by exerting its anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce inflammation in the reproductive organs and promote regular menstrual cycles. It relieves dysmenorrhea by acting as an analgesic and anxiolytic, thereby reducing pain and anxiety associated with menstrual cramps. Paeonia lactiflora improves ovulation through its antioxidant and anti-apoptotic properties, which help to prevent oxidative stress and cell death in the ovaries, leading to improved reproductive function. Moreover, it enhances fertility by stimulating the production of gonadotropin-releasing hormone, thus promoting the maturation of oocytes and subsequent ovulation. Additionally, Paeonia lactiflora reduces endometriosis by inhibiting the growth of endometrial cells and promoting the apoptosis of endometrial cells, thereby alleviating symptoms associated with endometriosis, and it also cures polycystic ovary syndrome by regulating insulin sensitivity and reducing androgen production, thus promoting ovulation and regular menstrual cycles, and supports reproductive health by acting as an adaptogen, thereby promoting balance and homeostasis in the reproductive system.
benefits_respiratory_system_description: Paeonia lactiflora alleviates coughs by suppressing mucus production, which helps to eliminate irritants causing the cough. Its anti-inflammatory properties relax airways, reducing constriction and allowing for smoother breathing. This relaxation also eases inflammation in the respiratory tract, reducing swelling and allowing for better airflow. Paeonia lactiflora prevents infections by exhibiting antimicrobial properties, inhibiting the growth of harmful bacteria and viruses. By soothing throats and calming allergies, Paeonia lactiflora also reduces congestion, heals tissues, and improves breathing, ultimately promoting optimal respiratory function.
benefits_urinary_system_description: Paeonia lactiflora alleviates kidney inflammation due to its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and pain in the kidneys. It supports urine production by stimulating the kidneys to filter waste and excess fluids from the blood more efficiently, thanks to its diuretic properties. The plant relieves bladder spasms with its antispasmodic properties, preventing involuntary contractions and cramps in the bladder. Paeonia lactiflora also prevents kidney stones by inhibiting the growth of oxalate crystals in the kidneys, and treats urinary tract infections with its antimicrobial properties, which combat bacterial growth in the urinary tract. Additionally, it reduces prostate swelling with its anti-androgenic properties, cures enuresis disorder by regulating bladder control, eases dysuria symptoms by relaxing the muscles in the urinary tract, prevents nocturia episodes by reducing urine production at night, and alleviates hematuria conditions by reducing bleeding in the urinary tract.
properties_desc: The medicinal properties of Paeonia lactiflora are multifaceted, with demonstrated antimicrobial activity attributed to the presence of phenolic acids and alkaloids, which inhibit the growth of bacteria and fungi. Paeonia lactiflora's antioxidant properties, mediated by flavonoids and phenolic acids, play a crucial role in neutralizing free radicals and protecting against oxidative stress. The anti-inflammatory effects of this plant are thought to be mediated by glycosides and sesquiterpenes, which inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and enzymes. Additionally, Paeonia lactiflora has been shown to possess analgesic and antispasmodic properties, attributed to the presence of alkaloids and volatile oils, which help to alleviate pain and muscle spasms. The sedative, antipyretic, and cardioprotective properties of Paeonia lactiflora, mediated by flavonoids and phenolic acids, contribute to its potential as a therapeutic agent for the treatment of various cardiovascular and nervous system disorders.

properties_list.description: Paeonia lactiflora has been found to exhibit antimicrobial activity, primarily against Gram-positive bacteria, through the inhibition of bacterial growth and the disruption of cell membrane integrity.

The plant contains antioxidants such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to scavenge free radicals and protect against oxidative stress, potentially reducing the risk of chronic diseases.

Paeonia lactiflora has anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to the presence of paeoniflorin and other compounds that inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and enzymes, thereby reducing inflammation.

The plant has been traditionally used as an analgesic, and studies have confirmed its ability to reduce pain by inhibiting the production of pain-related neurotransmitters and modulating the activity of pain-sensing neurons.
Paeonia lactiflora has been found to exhibit antispasmodic activity, which is attributed to the presence of paeoniflorin and other compounds that relax smooth muscle and reduce spasms.

The plant has been traditionally used as a sedative, and studies have confirmed its ability to induce sedation by modulating the activity of neurotransmitters such as GABA and serotonin, thereby reducing anxiety and promoting relaxation.

Paeonia lactiflora has been found to exhibit antipyretic activity, which is attributed to the presence of paeoniflorin and other compounds that reduce fever by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and enzymes.

The plant has been found to have cardioprotective effects, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids that reduce oxidative stress, inflammation, and platelet aggregation, thereby protecting against cardiovascular diseases.

constituents.description: Flavonoids from Paeonia lactiflora have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and free radical scavenging properties.
Saponins, found in Paeonia lactiflora, are a group of glycosides with hemolytic, foaming, and surfactant properties.
Phenolic acids are a class of compounds that exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, and have been found to possess chelating and radical scavenging abilities.
Paeonia lactiflora alkaloids exhibit anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties.
Paeonia lactiflora contains glycosides, which have anti-inflammatory, sedative, and vasodilatory properties.
Coumarins have anticoagulant, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties.
Paeonia lactiflora contains Triterpenoids, which exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory properties.
Tannins are astringent compounds with antiseptic, antimicrobial, and anti-inflammatory properties.
Anthocyanins are water-soluble, glycosidic, flavonoid pigments with antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and potentially antimicrobial properties.
Terpenoids are sesquiterpenoid compounds with anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties.

constituents_desc: The main medicinal constituents of Paeonia lactiflora are flavonoids, such as paeoniflorin and albiflorin, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects due to their ability to scavenge free radicals and inhibit pro-inflammatory enzymes. Saponins, including paeonoside and benzoylpaeoniflora saponin, exhibit immunomodulatory and anti-inflammatory activities, while phenolic acids, including caffeic acid and ferulic acid, demonstrate antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. Alkaloids, including paeoniline and paeoniline N-oxide, show anti-inflammatory and immunosuppressive activities, and glycosides, including paeoniflorin and benzoylpaeoniflora glycoside, display anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. These constituents are responsible for the medicinal properties of Paeonia lactiflora, which has been used in traditional Chinese medicine to treat various conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and inflammation. The bioactive compounds in Paeonia lactiflora have been extensively studied and are known to interact with multiple cellular pathways to exert their therapeutic effects.
parts_desc: The most used parts of Paeonia lactiflora for medicinal purposes are roots, flowers, and rhizomes. The roots contain paeoniflorin, paeonol, and tannins, which are primarily responsible for their anti-inflammatory, analgesic, and antispasmodic properties. The flowers contain flavonoids, such as kaempferol and quercetin, along with volatile oils, which contribute to their antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and sedative effects. The rhizomes are rich in paeoniflorin and benzoylated saponins, which exhibit anti-inflammatory, analgesic, and antipyretic properties. These bioactive compounds and associated medicinal properties make Paeonia lactiflora a valuable resource for traditional medicine, particularly in treating pain, inflammation, and fever.

parts_list.description: The roots of this plant contain major bioactive compounds including paeoniflorin, paeonol, and paeonilactone B, which exhibit anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and sedative properties, providing relief from pain, fever, and anxiety, while also supporting cardiovascular health and immune system function.

The flowers of this plant contain major bioactive compounds including paeoniflorin, benzylisoquinoline alkaloids, and flavonoids, which exhibit anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antioxidant properties, providing relief from pain, fever, and allergic reactions, while also supporting immune system function and cardiovascular health.

The rhizomes of this plant contain major bioactive compounds including paeoniflorin, paeonol, and alkaloids, which exhibit anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and sedative properties, providing relief from pain, fever, and anxiety, while also supporting cardiovascular health, immune system function, and digestive system health.

preparations.description: Tincture made from this plant is used to treat anxiety, insomnia, and restlessness, and also as a sedative and antispasmodic.
preparations.overview: Paeonia lactiflora tincture is a herbal remedy used to treat a range of conditions including anxiety, menstrual cramps, insomnia, premenstrual syndrome symptoms, depression, inflammation, stress, digestive issues, rheumatism and pain. It's a very commonly used preparation, often found in traditional Chinese medicine. The tincture is known to induce drowsiness as a common effect, making it a popular choice for insomnia. Paeonia lactiflora tincture is typically made by extracting the active compounds from the plant's roots and flowers using a solvent. The preparation can be moderately difficult to make, requiring careful attention to the ratio of plant material to solvent and the length of the extraction process.
recipe: Gather 1 cup of Paeonia lactiflora roots, 1/2 cup of Paeonia lactiflora flowers, and 2 cups of 80-proof vodka.
Combine the Paeonia lactiflora roots and flowers in a clean glass jar, and cover them with the vodka.
Steep the mixture in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily to facilitate infusion.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass container, discarding the solids.
Transfer the tincture to a dark glass bottle and store it in a cool, dark place, using 20-30 drops (0.5-0.75 mL) per serving as needed.

preparations.description: Decoction made from this plant is used to treat inflammation, fever, and menstrual disorders due to its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties.
preparations.overview: Paeonia lactiflora decoction is a herbal preparation used to treat inflammation, fever, anxiety, rheumatism, menstrual cramps, menstrual pain, eczema, digestive issues, headache, and rheumatoid arthritis. It's a common treatment in traditional medicine, widely used in many countries. Its effects include reduced inflammation, fever reduction, and relief from anxiety and pain. The decoction is made by boiling roots, rhizomes, and flowers of the Paeonia lactiflora plant. Preparation requires some expertise, as it demands precise measurements and boiling times, making it moderately difficult to make at home.
recipe: Gather 10 grams of dried Paeonia lactiflora roots and rhizomes, and 5 grams of dried Paeonia lactiflora flowers.
Clean and wash the ingredients thoroughly to remove any impurities.
Combine the dried ingredients in a pot and add 1 liter of water to create a decoction.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat and simmer for 30 minutes to allow the ingredients to infuse.
Strain the decoction and take 20-30 milliliters, 2-3 times a day, as needed, under the guidance of a healthcare professional.

preparations.description: Salve made from this plant is used to treat eczema, inflammation, itching, and pain, and to promote wound healing.
preparations.overview: Paeonia lactiflora salve is used to treat wounds, bruises, skin irritation, burns, inflammation, insect bites, muscle pain, sprains, eczema, and minor cuts. It's a commonly used herbal preparation in traditional medicine. This salve has a calming effect on the skin, reducing inflammation and promoting healing. It's typically made from the roots, rhizomes, and flowers of the Paeonia lactiflora plant, which require careful harvesting and processing to extract their medicinal properties. The preparation process can be time-consuming and labor-intensive due to the need for precise measurement and combination of ingredients, as well as the risk of contamination or degradation of the plant materials.
recipe: Harvest 1 cup of Paeonia lactiflora roots and rhizomes, and 1/2 cup of dried flowers.
Combine the roots and rhizomes in a saucepan, add 2 cups of water, and bring to a boil before reducing heat and simmering for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a bowl, discarding the solids and reserving the liquid.
Combine the reserved liquid with 1/2 cup of dried flowers and 1/4 cup of beeswax in a saucepan, and heat until the wax is melted.
Pour the mixture into a jar, and let it cool and solidify before using as a salve in a dosage of 1-2 teaspoons as needed.

preparations.description: Tea made from this plant is used to treat rheumatism, inflammation, and fever, and to improve digestion and menstrual irregularities.
preparations.overview: Paeonia lactiflora tea is a herbal remedy used to treat menstrual cramps, anxiety, insomnia, digestive issues, stress, depression, skin inflammation, pain relief, PMS, and PMS symptoms. It's a very common preparation used in traditional Chinese medicine due to its widespread availability and ease of use. Paeonia lactiflora tea often causes drowsiness due to its sedative properties. The roots and flowers of the Paeonia lactiflora plant are used to make this tea, which can be easily prepared by steeping the dried roots and flowers in hot water.
recipe: Gather 1 teaspoon of dried Paeonia lactiflora roots and 1 teaspoon of dried Paeonia lactiflora flowers.
Combine the roots and flowers in a tea infuser or a muslin bag.
Heat 8 ounces of water to 212°F and steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and discard the solids, then drink 1 cup per serving, up to 3 times a day.
Store any leftover tea in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours.

preparations.description: Capsule made from this plant is used to treat menstrual disorders, anxiety, insomnia, and as an antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory agent.
preparations.overview: Paeonia lactiflora capsule is widely used to treat anxiety, depression, menstrual cramps, inflammation, insomnia, digestive issues, stress, menstrual pain, PMS, and fever. It's one of the most commonly used herbal preparations in traditional medicine due to its versatility and efficacy. The most common effect of Paeonia lactiflora capsule is its ability to reduce anxiety and promote relaxation. The capsule is typically made from the roots and flowers of the Paeonia lactiflora plant, which are rich in alkaloids and glycosides that have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The process of making Paeonia lactiflora capsule involves a relatively straightforward extraction and drying process, but it requires some expertise and equipment to ensure the quality and potency of the final product.
recipe: Gather 30g of Paeonia lactiflora roots and 10g of Paeonia lactiflora flowers.
Dry and grind the roots and flowers into a fine powder.
Mix the powdered roots and flowers in a 3:1 ratio to create the herbal blend.
Fill gelatin capsules with the herbal blend, making sure each capsule contains 300mg of the mixture.
Store the herbal capsules in a cool, dry place and consume 1-2 capsules, 2-3 times a day, as needed.

preparations.description: Ointment made from this plant is used to treat eczema, dermatitis, and other skin conditions by reducing inflammation and promoting healing, and is also applied topically for pain relief and to aid in wound healing.
preparations.overview: Paeonia lactiflora ointment is a herbal preparation used to treat various skin conditions, including eczema, burns, bruises, psoriasis, wounds, insect bites, sprains, acne, and skin irritation. This preparation is widely used in traditional medicine due to its availability and effectiveness. Paeonia lactiflora ointment has anti-inflammatory properties, which are commonly observed in its application. The preparation is made from the roots, rhizomes, and flowers of the Paeonia lactiflora plant, which requires specific cultivation and harvesting techniques. The process of creating this ointment is labor-intensive and time-consuming, as it involves multiple extraction and purification steps to produce a stable and effective final product.
recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh Paeonia lactiflora roots and rhizomes, clean and dry them.
Combine the dried roots and rhizomes with 50g of dried Paeonia lactiflora flowers in a mortar.
Add 200ml of carrier oil, such as olive or jojoba oil, to the mortar and grind the mixture into a fine paste.
Strain the paste through a cheesecloth into a clean container, discard the solids, and mix in 5ml of vitamin E oil.
Store the Paeonia lactiflora herbal ointment in a cool, dark place and use 2-3 times a day as needed, applying a thin layer to the affected area.

preparation_desc: The most used herbal preparation of Paeonia lactiflora for medicinal purposes are the tincture, a concentrated liquid extract used to treat anxiety and insomnia due to its sedative properties, while decoction, a strong tea made by boiling roots or flowers, is used to alleviate menstrual cramps and other gynecological issues. The salve, a topical ointment, is applied to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain caused by wounds and burns. In addition, the tea, a liquid made by infusing dried flowers or roots in hot water, is used to treat digestive issues and as a diuretic. Paeonia lactiflora is also available in capsule form, which is used to treat pain and inflammation. The ointment, a topical medication, is used to treat skin conditions and reduce pain and inflammation caused by injuries.

side_effects.side_effect_description: Consuming Paeonia lactiflora can cause central nervous system depression resulting in sedation.
Paeonia lactiflora contains isoquinoline alkaloids which can cause respiratory depression by depressing the central nervous system
Paeonia lactiflora contains peonidin which causes blood pressure drop on standing
Paeonia lactiflora ingestion causes gastrointestinal symptoms including nausea.
Paeonia lactiflora contains iridoid glycosides which are known to have a laxative effect.
Consuming Paeonia lactiflora can cause emetic symptoms.
Paeonia lactiflora use is associated with elevated liver enzymes in some individuals.
Paeonia lactiflora contains cardiac glycosides that decrease heart rate
Paeonia lactiflora root extract causes a decrease in physical activity and energy
Paeonia lactiflora interacts with blood thinners, affecting anticoagulant therapy.

side_effects_overview: The possible side effects of improperly using Paeonia lactiflora are experience increased sedation, develop respiratory depression, experience nausea, and suffer from orthostatic hypotension due to its sedative and vasodilatory properties. Experience vomiting may also occur, particularly when consumed in large quantities or combined with other medications, which can lead to develop diarrhea and interacted with other medications. Moreover, prolonged use or excessive consumption can have elevated liver enzymes and experience bradycardia. Additionally, fatigue and drowsiness may become severe and persistent, contributing to a range of debilitating side effects. Develop respiratory depression, a potentially life-threatening condition, can also occur if Paeonia lactiflora is used improperly.

precautions.precaution_description: Paeonia lactiflora should be used under medical supervision due to potential interactions with medications.
Assess sensitivity to Paeonia lactiflora before use to avoid allergic reactions.
Paeonia lactiflora contains salicylic acid, a blood thinner that may interact with anticoagulant medications, causing a decrease in blood pressure.
Paeonia lactiflora contains emodin and other glycosides that may stimulate uterine contractions.
Paeonia lactiflora contains compounds that can pass into breast milk.
Paeonia lactiflora contains peylogenin, which is toxic to children.
Paeonia lactiflora may interact with blood thinners, such as warfarin, and increase the risk of bleeding.
Monitor patients for anaphylaxis, angioedema, and other allergic reactions

precautions_overview: The precautions to take before using Paeonia lactiflora medicinally are to consult a doctor, especially when considering its use for medicinal purposes, determine one's allergy status to the plant, as allergic reactions can occur. Monitoring blood pressure is also essential, as Paeonia lactiflora may lower blood pressure and interact with blood pressure medications. Furthermore, it is crucial to avoid using Paeonia lactiflora during pregnancy, as its safety has not been established, and breastfeeding is also not recommended due to the potential effects on the infant. Additionally, Paeonia lactiflora should be avoided in children, as there is limited information on its safety and efficacy in this population, and be aware of potential drug interactions, such as with anticoagulants and diabetes medications. Lastly, report any side effects to a healthcare provider immediately, as they can be a sign of a more serious reaction.
identification: Paeonia lactiflora is a perennial herbaceous plant that belongs to the Paeoniaceae family, characterized by palmate leaves with 5-9 leaflets and showy, terminal flowers with numerous, showy petals and stamens. It typically grows to a height of 1-2 meters, with a spread of around 60-90 cm, and has a rhizomatous root system. Native to China, this plant is also cultivated and naturalized in other parts of Asia, as well as in Europe and North America, where it thrives in moist, well-drained soils and full sun to partial shade. The plant's flowers, which can be white, pink, or purple, often have a strong, sweet fragrance and are a popular ornamental choice. In addition to its ornamental value, Paeonia lactiflora is also valued for its attractive foliage and ability to attract pollinators.
cultivation: Paeonia lactiflora is a perennial plant that grows up to 4-6 feet tall and 3-4 feet wide, forming a clump over time, with new growth emerging from the crown. It prefers well-draining, rich soil with a pH between 6.0-7.0 and full sun to partial shade, but can tolerate a range of conditions with some protection from extreme temperatures. To promote healthy growth, it's essential to provide Paeonia lactiflora with regular watering, especially during its first year of growth, and fertilize lightly in the spring with a balanced fertilizer. Dividing and replanting every 3-4 years can help maintain the plant's vigor and encourage new blooms, and removing spent flowers can also promote repeat blooming. By following these guidelines and providing the right environment, Paeonia lactiflora can thrive and produce vibrant flowers for years to come.
history: Paeonia lactiflora is a traditional Chinese medicinal plant that has been used for centuries to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, with its root being used to make a tea or decoction to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain, while its flower is used in combination with other herbs to treat menstrual disorders and promote lactation in new mothers, its antiseptic and antibacterial properties have also made it useful in treating wounds and abscesses, in addition, its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties have made it a popular treatment for headaches and other painful conditions.
